summary: PR #2282 decompiled five small functions across gmregclear, grcastle, groldpupupu, and tydisplay, updated recovered prototypes in headers, added one forward include for Item_GObj, and introduced small recovered data/struct access needed by the new matches. The PR body claims successful ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches; the bot report records five new 100% matches totaling +564 matched bytes and a small .sdata improvement.

key_files.role: Decompiled fn_8017DE54 and gm_80180BA0; refined lbl_80472E48_t layout by replacing trailing padding with x14[0x1B].
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes for fn_8017DE54 and gm_80180BA0 with recovered signatures.
Decompiled fn_801CFAFC with Ground castle union access and ftLib calls.
Added melee item forward include and recovered fn_801CFAFC prototype.
Decompiled grOldPupupu_80211C1C; added grOp_804D48E0 static table; uses PAD_STACK(8).
Decompiled un_8031BB34 as an index-to-joint-name string table lookup with -1 mapped to index 0.

what_changed: Replaced five decomp placeholders with C implementations: fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, grOldPupupu_80211C1C, and un_8031BB34.
Updated headers so recovered bodies have matching public prototypes instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Recovered part of gmregclear's lbl_80472E48_t layout by introducing x14[0x1B] at offset 0x14 while preserving sizeof(struct lbl_80472E48_t) == 0x80.
Added a local static grOp_804D48E0[1][2] table in groldpupupu.c; bot report records a .sdata improvement for groldpupupu.
Kept tydisplay asset/joint-name strings inline inside a local table for un_8031BB34 rather than turning them into global string symbols.
The bot report records five new 100% function matches: groldpupupu grOldPupupu_80211C1C, gmregclear fn_8017DE54 and gm_80180BA0, grcastle fn_801CFAFC, and tydisplay un_8031BB34.
smart_moves: Function bodies were paired with header prototype updates, avoiding stale UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations after signature recovery.
gmregclear recovered a typed array field inside an existing static struct instead of continuing to treat the whole region after offset 0x0C as undifferentiated padding.
tydisplay kept ordinary joint-name string literals inline in a function-local table, aligning with the loaded no-string-literal-symbol-regression standard.
The small loop in gm_80180BA0 expresses a repeated 0x1B-element operation directly rather than leaving repeated generated blocks.
The PR includes narrow verification claims in the body and an automated bot report confirming the five new 100% matches.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a decompiled body proves a function signature, update the owning header in the same PR so callers and prototype checks see the recovered argument and return types.
Small static data or struct-layout recovery can be part of a function match when the changed code accesses that data directly, but the evidence should identify the touched section or struct field.
For asset or joint-name lookup functions, an inline local string table can be a valid code-match shape when the strings are ordinary resource names and no data-ownership evidence is presented.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Address-style function names retained for unrecovered semantics: fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, grOldPupupu_80211C1C, un_8031BB34.
naming_conventions.notes: No semantic rename was attempted in this PR.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Short role locals: gp, other_gp, gobj, item, pos, idx, count.
naming_conventions.notes: These names describe local roles without asserting broader semantic meaning.

naming_conventions.name_or_pattern: Unknown or partially known data remains address-style or generic: lbl_80472E48.x14, grOp_804D48E0, gv.unk fields.
naming_conventions.notes: Consistent with conservative naming when source meaning is not established by the PR evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a counted for loop over 0x1B entries in gm_80180BA0, calling gm_80164024 and writing scaled x14 data through gmMainLib_8015D06C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The loop has bot-reported 100% function match evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use pointer stepping in fn_8017DE54 with p = &arg1[1] and compare three bytes against 0x21 before returning 3 - count.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Supported as part of a bot-reported 100% match, but the PR does not explain why this local shape was necessary.
PAD_STACK is a matching tactic that should remain evidence-backed; here the PR body and bot report support the resulting match, but no separate rationale is given.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Cast a Ground castle union field address to s16* in fn_801CFAFC: *(s16*) &gp->gv.castle2.xC4 = 4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The access uses a known union arm but still requires a cast, suggesting the exact field type may not be fully recovered.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Return a string-table entry as s32 from un_8031BB34, with idx == -1 normalized to 0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The return type is supported by the decompiled function and match report, though the neighboring un_8031BB94 signature in the excerpt returns char*.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for grOp_804D48E0 to see whether the [1][2] static table shape or name was refined.
Search later PRs for lbl_80472E48_t or lbl_80472E48.x14 to see whether the inferred gmregclear struct fields received semantic names.
Search later PRs for fn_801CFAFC and gp->gv.castle2.xC4 to see whether the s16 cast was replaced by a typed field.
Search later PRs for un_8031BB34 and un_8031BB94 to compare string table return type/signature cleanup.
Search for PAD_STACK in groldpupupu to confirm whether the stack padding remained necessary or was removed by later cleanup.

notes: Strong evidence for changed files, function names, diff contents, merge metadata, and bot-reported match results.
No review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and review feedback cannot be inferred.
The PR body claims ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff; the bot report independently records five 100% new matches, but no raw build logs or objdiff artifacts are loaded.
PAD_STACK and the s16 field cast are visible in the diff; the slice does not include author rationale for why those exact shapes were required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover headers alongside decompiled bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2282 shows decompiled function bodies paired with owning-header prototype updates for fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, and fn_801CFAFC, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with recovered signatures.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK use in grOldPupupu_80211C1C was tied to a reported 100% match
accepted_candidate_records.text: grOldPupupu_80211C1C includes PAD_STACK(8); the PR body claims all functions are 100% objdiff matches and the bot report records grOldPupupu_80211C1C at 100%. Treat this as a narrow tactic with evidence, not a general style rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Conservative names retained for miscellaneous matched functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR retains address-style function and data names where broader semantics are not evidenced, while using short role locals such as gp, other_gp, gobj, idx, and count.

source_update_candidates.title: gmregclear contains recovered lbl_80472E48_t x14 array access used by gm_80180BA0
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2282 changed lbl_80472E48_t to include x14[0x1B] at offset 0x14 and gm_80180BA0 reads lbl_80472E48.x14[idx] in a 0x1B-entry loop.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible path fact about recovered local struct layout; should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: grcastle fn_801CFAFC signature depends on Item_GObj forward declaration
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2282 added <melee/it/forward.h> to grcastle.h and changed fn_801CFAFC to void fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32).
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible source path/header dependency fact; verify against current header organization before adding to source corpus.

source_update_candidates.title: tydisplay un_8031BB34 maps trophy display indices to ToyDsp joint-name strings
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2282 implemented un_8031BB34(s8 idx) with a local table of ToyDsp*_Top_joint names and maps idx == -1 to 0 before returning table[idx] cast to s32.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible function behavior/path fact; should be checked against current source and neighboring tydisplay functions.

rejection_notes: Do not infer a general approval of PAD_STACK from this PR; the loaded standards require narrow evidence, and the PR only supports this one matched function use.
Do not infer semantic meanings for lbl_80472E48.x14, grOp_804D48E0, or gv.unk fields beyond the visible accesses.
Do not promote the PR body's build/test claims as raw validation logs; the slice includes claims and a bot report, not full build artifacts.
No review feedback was loaded, so no reviewer preference or acceptance rationale should be recorded.
